options.text: Any curved shape
A closed figure formed by three or more line segments
An open figure made of rays
A figure with exactly two sides

quiz_questions.explanation: Polygons A Polygon is a closed figure formed by three or more Line segments (sides) that meet at their endpoints (vertices). The other options — 'Any curved shape', 'A figure with exactly two sides', and 'An open figure made of rays' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: How many sides does a hexagon have?
quiz_questions.explanation: Common polygons: triangle (3), quadrilateral (4), pentagon (5), hexagon (6), heptagon (7), octagon (8), nonagon (9), decagon (10). The answer is 6. The other options — '5', '7', and '8' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

options.text: Any polygon with all sides equal
A polygon that is both equilateral and equiangular
A polygon with at least 6 sides
A polygon with all right angles

quiz_questions.explanation: Regular Polygon A regular Polygon is both equilateral (all sides congruent) and equiangular (all angles congruent). The other options — 'Any polygon with all sides equal', 'A polygon with all right angles', and 'A polygon with at least 6 sides' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

options.text: At least one interior angle greater than 180°
All sides of different lengths
No vertices
All interior angles less than 180°

quiz_questions.explanation: A function is concave up when f''(x) > 0 (bowl shape, ∪) and concave down when f''(x) < 0 (cap shape, ∩). The answer is 'At least one interior angle greater than 180°'. The other options — 'All interior angles less than 180°', 'No vertices', and 'All sides of different lengths' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the perimeter of a polygon?

options.text: The longest diagonal
The sum of the lengths of all its sides
The area enclosed by the polygon
The number of sides

quiz_questions.explanation: Perimeter The perimeter of a Polygon is the sum of the lengths of its sides. Polygons A Polygon is a closed figure formed by three or more Line segments (sides) that meet at their endpoints (vertices). The other options — 'The area enclosed by the polygon', 'The longest diagonal', and 'The number of sides' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: How many sides does a decagon have?
quiz_questions.explanation: Common polygons: triangle (3), quadrilateral (4), pentagon (5), hexagon (6), heptagon (7), octagon (8), nonagon (9), decagon (10). The answer is 10. The other options — '12', '8', and '9' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
